merged_events.event_description: Diplomatic relations established when Jonathan Russell presented credentials as American Minister Plenipotentiary to Sweden, opening a U.S. Legation in Stockholm.

evidence.quote: Diplomatic relations were established on April 29, 1818, when Jonathan Russell presented his credentials as American Minister Plenipotentiary to Sweden.

merged_events.event_description: U.S. Legation elevated to Embassy status with the appointment of H. Freeman Matthews as the first U.S. Ambassador to Sweden.

evidence.quote: On September 20, 1947, H. Freeman Matthews was appointed to be the first U.S. Ambassador to Sweden.

merged_events.event_description: Harrison Freeman Matthews presented credentials as Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary, elevating the U.S. mission in Stockholm from a Legation to an Embassy.

evidence.quote: Harrison Freeman Matthews, Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1947-09-20. Credentials presented: 1947-12-05. Left post on 1950-05-24. Note: Commissioned during a recess of the Senate; recommissioned on December 9, 1947, after confirmation.

matched.notes: Exact match on date (1818-04-29) and status (Legation). Both describe Jonathan Russell presenting credentials as Minister Plenipotentiary to Sweden.

CSV date 1947-12-05 matches POCOM Event 2 (credential presentation date). RDCR Event 1 gives 1947-09-20, but this is explicitly an appointment date ('was appointed to be the first U.S. Ambassador'), which is a personnel event, not an institutional elevation date. Per source priority rules, since RDCR provides only an appointment date and not an explicit institutional date, POCOM's credential presentation date (1947-12-05) correctly governs. CSV follows the correct source priority.
